SongPilot AI Help & Documentation

Click any section to expand. Your complete guide to bridging Logic Pro and Suno AI.

Getting Started ▼

What is SongPilot AI?

SongPilot AI is a production command center that bridges Apple Logic Pro and Suno AI. It helps you design songs from concept to completion: define your musical DNA, generate chord progressions, craft AI-optimized lyrics, plan arrangements, export MIDI for Logic Pro, and compile ready-to-paste styles for Suno AI.

Setting Up Your Gemini API Key

AI features (lyrics generation, theory analysis, melody suggestions, arrangement planning) require a free Google Gemini API key. Your key stays in your browser and is never sent anywhere except Google's API.

1
Visit aistudio.google.com/apikey and sign in with your Google account.
2
Click "Create API Key" and copy it.
3
In SongPilot AI, click the API Key item in the sidebar (or the key icon), paste your key, and click Save Key.

Choosing a Production Strategy

The first decision in any project is your strategy. This determines which cards and tabs are visible and how the workflow is optimized:

• AI-First — Let Suno generate the full track, then pull stems into Logic Pro for mixing/mastering.

• Producer-First — Build the instrumental in Logic Pro, then use Suno for vocal generation.

• Ping-Pong — Iterate between Logic Pro and Suno for co-creation.

• Stems Assembly — Generate individual elements (drums, bass, vocals) as separate Suno prompts.

• Cover/Remix — Upload a reference track and generate variations.

• Section Builder — Build each section separately (required for songs over 4 minutes).

Quick Start

The fastest way to begin: use a genre template from the Welcome screen or the Templates item in the sidebar. One click fills all DNA, structure, instrumentation, and steering settings. It is your starting point, not your ceiling.

Tab-by-Tab Guide ▶

Setup Tab

Your project foundation. Enter the client brief (or leave blank for personal projects), select your production strategy, define the narrative/theme, and choose your workflow mode (Full Song, Remix, Cover, Extend, Vocal Only, Stems). The AI Analyze Brief button auto-fills the entire form from a pasted client story.

Song DNA Tab

The musical creative direction for your track. Two main areas:

• Creative Direction — Genre, era, mood, vibe, and vocal style. These shape the Suno Style Field.

• Theory Settings — Key, scale, BPM, time signature, and song structure.

Key features: AI Suggest fills theory settings based on your genre selection. Theory Check validates that your key/scale/BPM choices make sense for the genre. Style Blending mixes two genres with a ratio slider and generates era-accurate vocabulary.

Chords Tab

Generate chord progressions from your DNA settings. Features include: transpose up/down, play preview (audio playback of chords), undo changes, and export Full Song MIDI for Logic Pro. The Blueprint shows a visual song map with an energy arc across sections.

Sections Tab

Edit chords and settings per individual section (Verse, Chorus, Bridge, etc.). Generate section-specific chord variations. Run Theory Check per section. Export individual section MIDI files. Drag section blocks to reorder the arrangement.

Lyrics Tab

Write lyrics manually or use AI generation with Suno section tags automatically inserted. The inline tag palette lets you click to insert music control tags (vocal style, instruments, dynamics) at the cursor position. The Suno Prompting Guide in the sidebar provides a full formatting reference.

Melody Tab

AI melody suggestions based on your chord progression and genre. Vocal range validation ensures the melody sits comfortably for your chosen voice type. Export melody as MIDI for Logic Pro.

Arrange Tab

Plan your instrument arrangement per section using an interactive grid. Toggle which instruments play in each section. Generate a complete Logic Pro session plan with track layout, bus routing, and plugin chains.

Suno Tab

Fine-tune the final output with production keywords, instrumentation chips, and Suno performance dials (Energy, Complexity, Weirdness, Style Influence). Reference audio analysis for matching BPM/key from an existing track. The Compile Production Suite button generates your final Suno-ready output.

Logic Pro Workflow ▶

Exporting MIDI and Importing into Logic Pro

From the Chords tab, click Export Full Song MIDI to download a .mid file containing your chord progression. Each section is placed sequentially with proper timing based on your BPM and time signature. In Logic Pro, use File > Import > MIDI File or drag the .mid directly onto a track.

Using the Chord Track with Session Players

After importing the MIDI, select all chord regions and use Edit > Create Chord Track from Selection. Logic's Session Player instruments (Drummer, Bass Player, Keyboard Player) will follow the chord track automatically, giving you an instant band arrangement that matches your SongPilot AI progression.

Section Markers in MIDI

Exported MIDI files include section markers (Intro, Verse, Chorus, etc.) that appear in Logic's arrangement track. These help you navigate and align regions when building out the full production.

Recommended Logic Pro Project Setup

Use the DAW tab in Studio to generate a complete session plan. This includes track groupings (Rhythm, Bass, Harmony, Lead, Vocals, FX), bus routing diagrams, and per-track plugin chains using stock Logic plugins. Print it as your session setup sheet and follow the track order when building your project.

For Suno-generated audio: import at the session BPM. Use Flex Time (Polyphonic) if tempo does not match exactly. The Suno master stem typically benefits from a high-pass at 30Hz and slight de-essing.

Suno AI Workflow ▶

Using Compile Production Suite

Click the Compile Production Suite button (in the Suno tab) to generate two fields: the Suno Style Field and the Suno Lyrics Field. These are optimized for direct pasting into Suno.

1
Copy Style Field — Paste into Suno's "Style of Music" input. This contains genre, era, mood, vocal style, instruments, and production keywords.
2
Copy Lyrics Field — Paste into Suno's lyrics input. This contains your lyrics with section tags and inline music control tags.
3
Set Suno Sliders — Match the Weirdness and Style Influence values shown in your Suno tab. These map directly to Suno's Creativity and Style Strength sliders.

Workflow Modes

• Full Song — Standard generation, up to ~4 minutes.

• Remix — Upload audio + style prompt for a reinterpretation.

• Cover — Upload a reference track for Suno to reimagine.

• Extend — Continue from a previous generation. Essential for songs over 4 minutes.

• Vocal Only — Generate vocals over your instrumental (Producer-First strategy).

• Stems — Generate individual elements (drums, bass, melody, vocals) as separate tracks.

Suno Slider Guide

• Weirdness (Creativity) — Low (0-30%): safe, predictable. Mid (30-60%): balanced. High (60-100%): experimental.

• Style Influence (Strength) — Low: loose interpretation. Mid: balanced. High: strict adherence to your style description.

Recommended combos: Wedding/client song: Weirdness 15-25%, Influence 65-80%. Genre exploration: Weirdness 40-60%, Influence 40-50%. Experimental: Weirdness 70-90%, Influence 20-35%.

Keyboard Shortcuts ▶
Ctrl/Cmd + Z
Undo chord changes
Escape
Close any open modal
Enter
Submit input in tag fields and search

Most actions are triggered by clicking buttons in the interface. The sidebar provides quick access to all views and tools.

Tips & Tricks ▶

General

• Use Theory Check to validate that your musical choices (key, scale, BPM) make sense for the genre before generating.

• Genre templates are great starting points — they fill every field instantly. Customize from there.

• The Style Blending Engine creates unique genre fusions. Try mixing unexpected genres (e.g., Jazz + Trap) for distinctive sounds.

• The energy arc in the Blueprint helps plan dynamics — make sure your song has tension and release.

• Always export MIDI from the Chords tab before moving to Logic Pro. The MIDI includes section markers and chord voicings.

Suno Tips

• Generate 3-5 variants every time. Rate them in the Take Vault to track what works.

• If a generation is 80% right, use Suno's "Remix" on that output rather than starting fresh.

• For wedding/event songs: keep Weirdness low (15-25%) and Style Influence high (65-80%).

• Always include a vocal style description in the Style field: "warm tenor", "breathy soprano", "raspy baritone".

• Keep the Style field under ~200 words. More is not better — Suno can get confused with too much detail.

Logic Pro Tips

• Import Suno output at the session BPM. Use Flex Time > Polyphonic if tempo does not match.

• Use Quick Sampler to chop individual Suno phrases for rearranging.

• The Suno master stem usually needs a high-pass at 30Hz and slight de-essing.

• Use the Style Advisor (sidebar) to compare two styles or get AI-suggested tweaks when a generation is close but not right.

• Client Brief + AI Analyze fills 90% of the form in one click — the fastest path from concept to output.

1 Vocal Identity
2 Song Blueprint

Plan your song's narrative before writing. Based on the SongTown Blueprint method — define what each part communicates, not what it says.

3 Section Intent Map

Define what each section communicates and its emotional character. Stable = resolved, certain. Unstable = tense, searching. (Pat Pattison prosody method)

4 Rhyme & Prosody

Set rhyme patterns and line structure. These guide both AI generation and manual writing.

5 Lyric DNA Summary & Export
Fill in the cards above to build your Lyric DNA profile.

Universal AI Brief compiles Song DNA + Lyric DNA into a prompt you can paste into Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, or any AI for lyrics, feedback, or collaboration.

1 Lyrics & Vocal
🎤 Male, Clean
⚠ Settings changed since lyrics were generated:
2 Suno Prompting Guide
Style Field — What Goes There

The Style field drives the music (not lyrics). Pack it with genre, instruments, vocal style, mood, and production descriptors.

indie folk, acoustic guitar, warm male tenor, fingerpicked, intimate, lo-fi warmth, gentle brush drums, upright bass, campfire feel
Inline Tags — Control Music From Lyrics

Place tags on their own line or inline with lyrics. Suno reads them as musical directions.

Structure[Verse] [Chorus] [Bridge] [Outro] [Intro] [Interlude] [Break] [Drop]
Voice[Soft] [Whispered] [Belting] [Falsetto] [Harmonies] [A Cappella] [Spoken Word] [Rap]
Instruments[Piano] [Acoustic Guitar] [Electric Guitar] [Strings] [Full Band] [Stripped Back] [Drums Enter]
Dynamics[Building] [Crescendo] [Fade Out] [Key Change] [Tempo Change] [Bass Drop]
Solos[Guitar Solo] [Fiddle Solo] [Piano Interlude] [Saxophone Break] [Drum Solo]
Pro Techniques

Stack tags for combined effect: [Chorus][Full Band, Belting, Harmonies]

Instrumental breaks: Put the tag alone on a line with no lyrics after it.

Vocal layering: [Harmonies] adds backing vocals. [Duet] adds a second singer.

Energy management: Start verses with [Soft] or [Stripped Back], build to [Full Band] at the chorus.

The extend trick: End your lyrics mid-phrase to force Suno to continue when you use Extend. End with [Fade Out] or [End] for a clean finish.

Suno Sliders — Quick Reference
SliderLowMid (50%)High
WeirdnessSafe, predictableNormal baselineChaos, experimental deviations
Style InfluenceLoose interpretationBalancedStrict adherence to style prompt
Audio InfluenceVibe onlyMelodic referenceClosely match uploaded audio

Radio Pop: Weirdness 35–50%, Style 65–80%. Experimental: Weirdness 70–85%, Style 35–55%. Client work: Weirdness 25–40%, Style 70–85%. Audio Influence only appears when you upload a reference clip.

Tip: High Weirdness during Extend causes style drift. Keep it below 50% for extends. Style Influence plateaus above ~80%.

Full Example — Wedding Song
[Intro][Soft, Acoustic Guitar, Fingerpicked] [Verse][Warm, Intimate] From the moment that you smiled I knew this day would come Standing here beside your groom My little girl, all grown [Acoustic Guitar Solo] [Chorus][Full Band, Harmonies, Emotional] And I'm so proud of who you are You light up every room Daddy's girl will always be The best part of my tune [Bridge][Soft, Piano, Strings] Time goes by too fast they say But every day with you was gold [Chorus][Belting, Full Band, Big Finish] And I'm so proud of who you are... [Outro][Fade Out, Acoustic Guitar]
Negative Prompting (v5)

Suno v5 supports negative prompts in the Style field using "no X" syntax. Add unwanted elements at the end of your style text to steer the AI away from them.

indie folk, acoustic guitar, warm male tenor, no autotune, no heavy reverb, no electronic drums

Suno Pro and Premier plans also have an Exclude Styles toggle in the UI that lets you explicitly block style elements. SongPilot AI's "Keep Out" field automatically converts your exclusions into "no X" entries in the compiled Style field.

Personas (v5)

Personas are reusable vocal identities in Suno v5. Create a Persona from any song you like, then apply it to new generations for consistent vocal character across an entire album or project. Great for maintaining a signature sound across multiple tracks.

v5 Features

Studio Mode: Edit individual sections of a generated song without re-generating the whole track. Tweak a verse, swap a chorus, or refine a bridge independently.

Stem Export: Export up to 12 individual stems (vocals, drums, bass, keys, etc.) for mixing in your DAW. Available on Pro and Premier plans.

Sample-to-Song: Upload an audio sample (melody hum, guitar riff, beat) and Suno builds a full song around it. Use the Audio Influence slider to control how closely the output follows your sample.

⚡ Quick Actions

📊 Progress

👤 Client Manager

Manage your clients and their song projects. Click a client to see their tracks.

🎨 Suno Styles

Your library of proven Suno Style strings. Save what works, reuse it on future projects.

How to build your library:
From Take Vault: After a great Suno result, save the Style from your Take Vault — it captures the exact Style string + settings.
Paste from Suno: Copy Suno's AI-enhanced Style after it improves your prompt, then click "Paste New Style" to save it here.
Save Current: Saves whatever is currently in your compiled Style field + Weirdness/Influence sliders.
🔍 Style Advisor

Troubleshoot or learn from Suno's Style improvements.

Three ways to use:
Troubleshoot: Paste your Style in A, describe the problem in B → AI suggests specific tweaks.
Learn from Suno: Paste YOUR Style in A, paste SUNO'S enhanced version in B → see what Suno changed and why.
Compare: Paste two different Styles to see the differences side-by-side.
Suno AI Complete Reference

Click any tag to copy. Tags go in [brackets] in lyrics, or as plain text in the Style field. Color = category.

💡 Looking for lyric structure planning? Use the Lyric DNA tab in Studio for blueprints, rhyme schemes, and section intents.

Style Field vs Lyrics Tags
STYLE FIELD (plain text, no brackets)

Sets the global sound. Suno reads this first.

Use: genre + era + mood + texture + vocal type

Atmospheric indie folk, warm analog, male vocal, fingerpicked acoustic guitar, intimate

LYRICS FIELD (brackets inline)

Controls local changes at specific moments.

Use: section markers + instrument changes + vocal shifts + transitions

[Verse][Soft, Breathy]
Lyrics here...
[Acoustic Guitar Solo]
[Chorus][Belting, High Energy]

Slider Intelligence

Recommended slider settings based on your current genre. These map to Suno's More Options panel.

Key rules: Change one slider at a time. Run 2-4 variants per change. Weirdness has strongest impact during Extend. Style Influence plateaus above ~80%. Audio Influence only appears when you upload audio.
Inline Lyrics Tag Builder

Build a lyrics block with embedded control tags. Click tags to insert at cursor. This is what you paste into Suno's lyrics field.

Preview (Suno-ready)

                    

                


                

                    
Your Workflow: Demo → Suno → Logic Pro

                    

                        

                            1.Client gives you: lyrics, demo recording, piano melody, or chord sheet
                            2.In SongPilot AI: Pick strategy (Producer-First or Ping-Pong). Fill DNA from their demo. Use Chord Engine for section progressions.
                            3.Build lyrics with tags: Use the Inline Builder above. Add [section], [instrument], and [vocal] tags between their lyrics.
                            4.In Suno — Style field: Paste the compiled Style from SongPilot AI (genre, mood, texture, vocal).
                            5.In Suno — Lyrics field: Paste the tagged lyrics from the Inline Builder.
                            6.In Suno — Upload audio: Upload their demo/piano melody. Set Audio Influence 60-80% if melody should lead, 20-40% for texture only.
                            7.Sliders: Use the genre-recommended settings above. Generate 3-5 variants.
                            8.In Logic Pro: Import best Suno output. Use Session Planner for track layout. Mix and master.
                        

                    

                

            

        


        
        

            

                

                    

                        
🌟 Templates

                        
                    

                    
Pre-built starting points that fill Setup + Song DNA instantly. Pick a template, customize from there. Built-in templates can't be deleted. Your custom templates appear under "My Templates".

                

                

            

        


        
        

            

                

                    
⚙️ Preferences — Default Settings

                    
Set your personal defaults for new projects. These apply every time you start a new song.


                    
                    
Producer Identity

                    

                        

                        

                    


                    
                    
Musical Defaults

                    

                        

                        

                        

                    

                    

                        

                        

                        

                    


                    
                    
Song Type Defaults

Help & Reference Guide

                        
                    

                    
Click any section to expand. Search to filter.

                


                

                    
                    

                        
Your First Song in 3 Minutes ▼

                        

                            
1
Setup Tab — Set project identity, song format (vocal/instrumental), duration, client brief, and reference songs. Pick a production strategy.

                            
2
Song DNA Tab — Define genre, key, BPM, structure, style, instruments, and sound direction. Use "AI Analyze Brief" to auto-fill or pick a Template from the sidebar.

                            
3
Lyric DNA Tab — Plan your lyrics: set POV, big idea, blueprint, section intents, rhyme scheme. Copy the Universal AI Brief to paste into any AI for lyrics.

                            
4
Lyrics Tab — Write or AI-generate Suno-tagged lyrics. All your Lyric DNA feeds into the AI prompt for focused, consistent output.

                            
5
Chords → Sections → Melody → Arrange — Build progressions, assign chords to sections, sketch melodies, plan your arrangement. Export MIDI and MusicXML for your DAW.

                            
6
Suno Tab — Compile everything into Suno's Style Field and Lyrics Field. Set sliders, review, and copy into Suno to generate.

                            
7
DAW Tab — Import Suno output, detect artifacts, generate a Logic Pro session plan, and export lyrics/chord charts for your session.

                        

                    


                    
                    

                        
Production Strategies Explained ▶

                        

                            
AI-First: Let Suno generate the full track. Pull stems into Logic Pro for mixing and mastering. Best for fast ideation or when you want AI to lead creatively.

                            
Producer-First: You build the instrumental in Logic Pro. Export a reference or stems. Use Suno specifically for vocal generation. Best when you have a strong musical vision.

                            
Ping-Pong: Sketch in Logic, generate variations in Suno, refine in Logic, extend in Suno. Iterative co-creation. Best for complex arrangements.

                            
Stems Assembly: Generate drums, bass, melody, and vocals as separate Suno prompts. Import each as its own Logic track. Maximum control over each element.

                            
Cover/Remix: Upload a reference track. Suno generates a variation. Enhance in Logic. Best for reinterpretation work.

                            
Section Builder: Generate each section (verse, chorus, bridge) as a separate Suno prompt with different settings. Stitch together in Logic. Required for songs over 4 minutes.

                        

                    


                    
                    

                        
Suno Style Field — What Goes In It ▶

                        

                            
The Style field tells Suno the overall sound. It's plain text, no brackets. Suno reads it as a description, not commands.

                            
What works well:

                            
Genre + era + mood + vocal description + key instruments. Example:

                            
Modern country, heartfelt, warm male vocal, acoustic guitar, pedal steel, fiddle, gentle brushed drums, Nashville production, slow dance tempo

                            
Tips:

                            
- Be specific: "warm male vocal with slight rasp" beats "male vocal"

                            
- Name instruments: "pedal steel guitar, fingerpicked acoustic" beats "country instruments"

                            
- Describe the feel: "intimate, living room performance" gives Suno a vibe to aim for

                            
- Keep under ~200 words. More isn't better — Suno can get confused with too much.

                        

                    


                    
                    

                        
Suno Lyrics Metatags — Controlling the Music ▶

                        

                            
These tags go inside the lyrics field in square brackets. They control structure, voice, and instruments.

                            
Structure Tags (start of each section):

                            
[Intro] [Verse] [Verse 2] [Pre-Chorus] [Chorus] [Post-Chorus] [Bridge] [Outro] [Interlude] [Break] [Drop] [Buildup] [End]

                            
Voice Control Tags (how the singer performs):

                            
[Soft] [Whispered] [Belting] [Falsetto] [Harmonies] [A Cappella] [Spoken Word] [Raspy] [Powerful] [Emotional] [Duet]

                            
Instrument/Music Tags (what plays):

                            
[Acoustic Guitar] [Electric Guitar] [Piano] [Strings] [Fiddle Solo] [Guitar Solo] [Drums Enter] [Full Band] [Stripped Back] [Bass Drop]

                            
Dynamic Tags (energy changes):

                            
[Building] [Crescendo] [Fade Out] [Fade In] [Key Change] [Tempo Change] [Breakdown]

                            
Example — Adding an acoustic solo before the chorus:

                            
[Verse][Soft, Acoustic Guitar]
She had a spark about her from the start
A laugh that could light up any room...

[Acoustic Guitar Solo]

[Chorus][Full Band, Belting, Harmonies]
And I'm so proud of who you are...

                        

                    


                    
                    

                        
Suno Sliders — Weirdness & Style Influence ▶

                        

                            
Weirdness (Suno calls it "Creativity")

                            
Controls how much Suno experiments vs plays it safe.

                            
- Low (0-30%): Safe, predictable, stays close to the genre. Good for: client work, wedding songs, commercial projects.

                            
- Mid (30-60%): Balanced. Some creative choices but still recognizable. Good for: most songs.

                            
- High (60-100%): Wild. Unexpected chord changes, unusual sounds, genre-bending. Good for: experimental, art projects.

                            
Style Influence (Suno calls it "Style Strength")

                            
Controls how strongly Suno follows your Style field description.

                            
- Low (0-30%): Loose interpretation. Suno takes creative liberties. Your style is a suggestion.

                            
- Mid (30-60%): Balanced adherence. Best default for most work.

                            
- High (60-100%): Strict adherence. Suno closely matches your description. Good for: specific client requests, matching a reference.

                            
Recommended combos:

                            
- Wedding/client song: Weirdness 15-25%, Influence 65-80%

                            
- Genre exploration: Weirdness 40-60%, Influence 40-50%

                            
- Experimental: Weirdness 70-90%, Influence 20-35%

                        

                    


                    
                    

                        
Working with Client Demos & Audio ▶

                        

                            
Scenario: Client gives you lyrics + a piano melody demo

                            
1
Paste the client's background in Client Brief Intake. Click "AI Analyze Brief" to fill DNA.

                            
2
In Reference Audio Engine, upload their demo MP3. Click "Analyze Audio" for BPM/key detection.

                            
3
Click "AI Write Lyrics" or paste their lyrics. Add inline Suno tags for music control.

                            
4
Compile. Copy the Style Field and Lyrics into Suno.

                            
5
In Suno, click "Upload Audio" and upload the piano demo. Set Audio Influence: 60-80% if the melody should lead, 20-40% if it's just a vibe reference.

                            
6
Generate 3-5 variants. Rate them in the Take Vault.

                            
7
Download the best Suno output. Import into Logic Pro. Use the Session Planner for track layout and plugin chains.

                            
Suno Audio Influence tips:

                            
- Piano/guitar melody you want to keep: 60-80%

                            
- Vocal demo for melody reference: 50-70%

                            
- Full demo for "vibe" only: 20-40%

                            
- Rhythm/groove reference: 30-50%

                        

                    


                    
                    

                        
Card-by-Card Reference ▶

                        

                            
Setup Tab

                            
• Project Identity: Artist name and track title.

                            
• Song Format: Vocal or Instrumental, target duration, vocal type/texture or lead instrument.

                            
• Client Brief: Paste the client's story. AI extracts everything.

                            
• Strategy & Workflow: Production approach and Suno creation mode.

                            
• Narrative: Creative theme and AI album art.

                            
Song DNA Tab

                            
• Musical DNA: Genre, structure, key, scale, BPM, time sig. Theory Check validates your choices.

                            
• Style: Era, mood, vibe. Era & genre vocabulary chips. Style blending engine.

                            
• Keywords: Sound descriptors for the Suno Style field. Genre-specific chip suggestions.

                            
• Instruments: Instrumentation with AI suggestions. Genre-specific chip suggestions.

                            
• Sound Direction: Must Include (what the song needs) and Keep Out (negative prompts).

                            
• Suno Style Preview: Live preview of the compiled Style field. Click chips to toggle on/off.

                            
Remaining Tabs

                            
• Lyrics: AI write/rewrite, polish, auto-tag. Click tags in lyrics to replace them.

                            
• Chords: Quick Generate, Smart Generate, Deep Theory. MIDI export.

                            
• Sections: Visual section timeline. Per-section chords, lyrics, energy.

                            
• Melody: AI melody generation with vocal range validation. MIDI export.

                            
• Arrange: Instrument × section grid. AI arrangement suggestions.

                            
• Production: Producer notes, reference audio engine, Suno sliders, Compile.

                        

                    


                    
                    

                        
Section Builder & the 4-Minute Limit ▶

                        

                            
Suno generates a maximum of ~4 minutes per generation. For longer songs, you need to build in segments.

                            
How to use the Section Builder:

                            
1
Set your structure in Musical DNA (e.g., Radio Edit). The Section Builder auto-creates timeline blocks.

                            
2
Click "Chords All" to generate chord progressions for every section based on genre and harmonic function.

                            
3
Click each section to edit: adjust energy, add section-specific Suno prompts, lyrics, and transitions.

                            
4
Click "Compile All" to generate one combined Suno-ready output with all sections tagged.

                            
The Extend Plan at the bottom shows how to chain Suno "Extend" calls: generate the first ~3:30, then use Suno's Extend feature to add the next section from where the previous one left off.

                        

                    


                    
                    

                        
Logic Pro Session Planner ▶

                        

                            
The Session Planner generates a complete Logic Pro X track layout based on your genre and strategy.

                            
What it creates:

                            
- Track list organized by group (Rhythm, Bass, Harmony, Lead, Vocals, FX)

                            
- Bus routing diagram (Drum Bus, Bass Bus, Synth Bus, Vocal Bus, Send FX, Mix Bus)

                            
- Per-track plugin chains using stock Logic plugins (specific Channel EQ settings, Compressor types, reverb presets)

                            
- Strategy-specific import tracks (e.g., "Suno Vocal Stem", "Suno Drum Stem" for AI-First workflow)

                            
Print it as your session setup sheet. Open Logic, create tracks in the listed order, apply the suggested plugins.

                            
Click "AI Deep Plan" for Gemini-powered detailed recommendations specific to your exact song.

                        

                    


                    
                    

                        
Pro Tips & Shortcuts ▶

                        

                            
Speed tricks:

                            
- Use Templates (sidebar) to instantly load genre presets instead of filling every field

                            
- Client Brief + AI Analyze fills 90% of the form in one click

                            
- Section Chords button in DNA generates chords for all sections at once

                            
- Prompt Diff (sidebar) compares two prompts or suggests tweaks when a generation is close but not right

                            
Suno tricks:

                            
- Generate 3-5 variants every time. Rate them in Take Vault.

                            
- If a generation is 80% right, use Suno's "Remix" on that specific output — not a fresh generation

                            
- For wedding/event songs: keep Weirdness low (15-25%) and Style Influence high (65-80%)

                            
- Always include vocal style in the Style field: "warm tenor", "breathy soprano", "raspy baritone"

                            
Logic Pro tricks:

                            
- Import Suno output at the session BPM. Flex Time > Polyphonic if tempo doesn't match exactly.

                            
- Use Quick Sampler to chop individual Suno phrases for rearranging

                            
- The Suno master stem usually needs high-pass at 30Hz and slight de-essing

                        

                    

                

            

        


        
        

            

                

                    

                        
Logic Pro Session Planner

                        

                            
                            
                            
                        

                    

                    
Auto-generated track layout, bus routing, and plugin chains based on your genre and strategy. Print this as your session setup sheet.
